_comment: Industry-specific application rates and material economics. Loaded by db/database.js seedMaterialsKB() at boot — UPSERT semantics so editing this file + redeploying refreshes the rows. Numbers are typical US industry ranges sourced from public manufacturer datasheets and trade-association rules of thumb. Region defaults to 'US'; per-region rows can be added with a region key. Keep keys stable — the AI prompts reference them by name.

rows.label: Pressure-washing exterior siding (residential, single story)
rows.source: Trade range: low for vinyl simple jobs, high for tall multi-story or heavy mildew

rows.label: Concrete flatwork (driveway, patio, pad)
rows.source: Higher end for oil/grease lots; commercial parking pads typically $0.10-$0.18

rows.label: Minimum call-out / mobilization fee
rows.source: Most operators won't roll a truck for less than $150-$250

rows.label: Asphalt sealcoat (refresh)
rows.source: PaveScape/SealMaster typical commercial rates; lower end requires economies of scale

rows.label: Coalcoat/sealer coverage per gallon (single coat, prepped surface)
rows.source: Manufacturer-published; older oxidized lots fall to 50-60

rows.label: Crack-fill (hot rubberized)
rows.source: Larger cracks (>1") and routing pushes toward the high end

rows.label: Re-stripe per parking stall (single 9'x18' stall, single line)
rows.source: Simple re-stripe; double-line and ADA stalls cost more

rows.label: New layout per stall (measure, layout, paint)
rows.source: New layout is 1.5-2.5x re-stripe; includes engineering/layout time

rows.label: ADA / handicap stall premium (vs. standard restripe)
rows.source: Includes blue paint, hatched access aisle, sign post stencil

rows.label: Linear footage rate (when bid by lin-ft instead of stalls)
rows.source: Used for curb paint, fire lanes, traffic arrows — all linear elements

rows.label: Exterior wall painting (2 coats, prep included)
rows.source: Stucco/wood/cedar pushes high; vinyl and EIFS lower

rows.label: Interior painting (typical, by floor area)
rows.source: Per floor area. Walls + ceiling. Vaulted/textured ceilings push high

rows.label: Paint coverage per gallon (latex, single coat, smooth surface)
rows.source: Manufacturer specs (Sherwin-Williams, Behr Premium); rough/textured drops to 150-200

rows.label: Asphalt shingle reroof, complete (per square = 100 sqft)
rows.source: Includes tear-off + underlayment + shingles + flashing. Steep pitch >8/12 adds 15-25%

rows.label: Tear-off only (single layer)
rows.source: Multi-layer tear-off adds $50-$80/square per additional layer

rows.label: Flat / low-slope recoat (silicone or acrylic)
rows.source: Silicone runs higher than acrylic; existing TPO/EPDM affects prep cost

rows.label: New concrete flatwork (4" thick, sub-base prep, finish)
rows.source: Stamped/colored pushes to $14-$20; commercial broom finish on grade is low end

rows.label: Concrete demo + haul-off
rows.source: Reinforced or thick (>6") slabs push high; access difficulty matters

rows.label: Sod install (St. Augustine / Bermuda, prepped subgrade)
rows.source: Higher for premium grasses (zoysia) and challenging access

rows.label: Hardwood mulch installed (3" depth)
rows.source: Color-enhanced and rubber mulch run higher
